evidence_text: The paper introduces a diagnostic framework that separately measures retrieval relevance, memory utilization, and failure modes in memory-augmented LLM agents.
source_excerpt: "We introduce a diagnostic framework that analyzes how performance differences manifest across write strategies, retrieval methods, and memory utilization behavior" and "propose a diagnostic probing framework that sits at the retrieval-to-generation boundary and independently measures retrieval relevance, memory utilization, and failure modes."
note: This is a framework description, not a performance result.
caveat: This is a framework description, not a performance result.

evidence_text: The evaluation uses a 3 × 3 factorial design crossing three write strategies with three retrieval methods.
citation: Yuan et al., Abstract; §1; Appendix B
source_excerpt: "We conduct a controlled 3 × 3 factorial study that crosses three write strategies (raw chunks, Mem0-style extraction, MemGPT-style summarization) with three retrieval methods (cosine similarity, BM25, hybrid reranking)." Appendix B also states, "We adopt a 3 × 3 factorial design that crosses three write strategies with three retrieval methods."
note: The source describes this as a controlled comparison design.
caveat: The source describes this as a controlled comparison design.

evidence_text: The study evaluates 1,540 non-adversarial questions from LoCoMo.
source_excerpt: "Evaluating on LoCoMo (1,540 non-adversarial questions)" and Appendix B states, "We evaluate on 1,540 non-adversarial questions."
note: The source also describes LoCoMo as 10 multi-session conversations with roughly 200 questions per conversation.
caveat: The source also describes LoCoMo as 10 multi-session conversations with roughly 200 questions per conversation.

evidence_text: Average accuracy varies from 57.1% to 77.2% across retrieval methods.
source_excerpt: The abstract states, "average accuracy spans 20 points across retrieval methods (57.1% to 77.2%)." Table 1 reports average accuracy of 73.4 for cosine, 57.1 for BM25, and 77.2 for hybrid.
note: The reported span is across retrieval methods, not across write strategies.
caveat: The reported span is across retrieval methods, not across write strategies.

evidence_text: Within a retrieval method, write strategy changes accuracy by only 3 to 8 percentage points.
source_excerpt: The abstract states, "average accuracy spans 20 points across retrieval methods (57.1% to 77.2%) but only 3–8 points across write strategies." Section 3.1 says, "write strategy accounts for only 3–8 accuracy points within any retrieval column."
note: This evidence compares within-column write-strategy spread, not end-to-end cost.
caveat: This evidence compares within-column write-strategy spread, not end-to-end cost.

evidence_text: Basic RAG stores raw 3-turn conversation chunks with speaker names and timestamps and requires zero LLM calls at write time.
citation: Yuan et al., §2.1; Appendix B Table 3
source_excerpt: "Basic RAG stores raw 3-turn conversation chunks with speaker names and timestamps, requiring zero LLM calls at write time." Appendix B Table 3 lists "Write Basic RAG Store raw 3-turn chunks None."
note: This evidence describes the write strategy configuration used in the experiment.
caveat: This evidence describes the write strategy configuration used in the experiment.

evidence_text: Basic RAG achieved 77.9% accuracy with cosine retrieval and 81.1% with hybrid retrieval.
source_excerpt: Table 1 reports "Basic RAG ... 77.9" under cosine accuracy and "81.1" under hybrid accuracy. Section 3.1 states, "Basic RAG ... achieves 77.9% under cosine and 81.1% under hybrid."
note: These are the reported accuracy values for the raw-chunk write strategy under two retrieval methods.
caveat: These are the reported accuracy values for the raw-chunk write strategy under two retrieval methods.

evidence_text: Under BM25, Summarized Episodes scored 62.7% accuracy versus 59.2% for Basic RAG.
source_excerpt: Section 3.1 states, "The one exception is BM25, where Summarized Episodes (62.7%) edges out Basic RAG (59.2%), likely because keyword retrieval benefits from the denser vocabulary of compressed summaries." Table 1 reports BM25 accuracy of 62.7 for Summ. Episodes and 59.2 for Basic RAG.
note: This is an exception to the broader pattern that raw chunk storage matches or exceeds the more expensive write strategies. The source gives only a likely explanation, not a directly established mechanism.
caveat: This is an exception to the broader pattern that raw chunk storage matches or exceeds the more expensive write strategies. The source gives only a likely explanation, not a directly established mechanism.

evidence_text: Retrieval precision and downstream accuracy are reported as near-perfectly correlated, with r = 0.98.
source_excerpt: "Retrieval precision is near-perfectly correlated with downstream accuracy (r = 0.98, Figure 4), providing strong evidence that retrieval quality most strongly correlates with downstream performance." The figure caption states, "Precision@5 vs. accuracy (r = 0.98)."
note: The source reports correlation, not causal proof.
caveat: The source reports correlation, not causal proof.

evidence_text: Retrieval failure accounted for 11% to 46% of questions across configurations.
source_excerpt: Section 3.3 states, "Retrieval failure is the dominant error mode, accounting for 11–46% of all questions depending on configuration." Table 2 shows retrieval failure rates ranging from 11.4 to 46.3 across the nine settings.
note: The upper end corresponds to BM25 with Extracted Facts; the lower end corresponds to Basic RAG with Hybrid.
caveat: The upper end corresponds to BM25 with Extracted Facts; the lower end corresponds to Basic RAG with Hybrid.

evidence_text: For Basic RAG, hybrid reranking reduced retrieval failure from 35.3% under BM25 to 11.4%.
source_excerpt: Section 3.3 states, "Hybrid reranking cuts retrieval failures roughly in half (e.g., 35.3% to 11.4% for Basic RAG)." Table 2 reports "Basic RAG BM25 ... Retrieval 35.3" and "Basic RAG Hybrid ... Retrieval 11.4."
note: This evidence is a configuration-specific failure-mode comparison.
caveat: This evidence is a configuration-specific failure-mode comparison.

evidence_text: Across configurations, utilization failures remained between 4% and 8%, and hallucinations remained between 0.4% and 1.4%.
source_excerpt: Section 3.3 states, "By contrast, utilization failures remain stable at 4–8% and hallucinations at 0.4–1.4% regardless of configuration." Table 2 lists utilization values from 3.9 to 8.1 and hallucination values from 0.4 to 1.4.
note: The text rounds utilization failures to 4–8%, while the table includes one 3.9% value.
caveat: The text rounds utilization failures to 4–8%, while the table includes one 3.9% value.

evidence_text: The study states that its conclusions are limited by use of a single backbone model, one benchmark, fixed retrieval budget k = 5, prompt-based write strategies, and LLM judges for correctness and failure classification.
citation: Yuan et al., Limitations; Appendix D
source_excerpt: "We introduce a diagnostic framework that analyzes how performance differences manifest across write strategies, retrieval methods, and memory utilization behavior" and "propose a diagnostic probing framework that sits at the retrieval-to-generation boundary and independently measures retrieval relevance, memory utilization, and failure modes."
note: This is a framework description, not a performance result.
caveat: This is a framework description, not a performance result.
